serving.natural_language_portion: One plate of cereal crispy tofu, typically served as a side dish or appetizer, weighs approximately 300 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore for deep-fried tofu and cereal-based dishes, considering the specific preparation method and ingredients.

gi_source.title: International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI estimated based on deep-fried tofu (GI 40) and cereal flakes (Nestum, GI 55-60), considering the overall carbohydrate content and preparation method. The presence of protein and fat from tofu and deep-frying moderates the overall glycemic response.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume this dish in moderation due to its sodium content (300mg/100g) and saturated fat (5.0g/100g), and consider requesting reduced salt and plant-based alternatives for butter.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate and sugar, low glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish has a low glycemic index (45) and moderate carbohydrate (13.33g/100g) and sugar (3.33g/100g) content, making it suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in controlled portions, although cereal flakes contain refined carbohydrates.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Due to its saturated fat (5.0g/100g) and cholesterol (50mg/100g) from deep-frying and dairy components, individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption and opt for air-fried versions or plant-based fats where possible.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Advanced Glycation End products (AGEs) from deep-frying
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Deep-frying generates AGEs which can exacerbate inflammation in rheumatoid arthritis; it is advisable to choose alternative cooking methods like air-frying or pan-frying to reduce inflammatory triggers.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cereal Crispy Tofu is a popular Zi Char dish, originating from Singaporean and Malaysian culinary traditions, featuring deep-fried tofu coated in a savory and slightly sweet mixture of cereal flakes, butter, curry leaves, and chilli padi. The tofu is typically crispy on the outside and soft on the inside, offering a delightful textural contrast. The cereal coating provides a unique umami flavor with hints of sweetness and spice. Nutritionally, it is a mixed dish with moderate energy (216.67 kcal/100g), protein (8.33g/100g), and fat (15.0g/100g), with a low glycemic index of 45. While tofu itself is a healthy protein source, the deep-frying process and cereal coating contribute to higher fat and carbohydrate content. It is vegetarian but contains dairy and gluten from the cereal and butter.
common_user_queries: How is cereal crispy tofu made?
